title: Reconnaissance Troop Transporter
content:

*Patrol *Search-and-destroy missions |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]][[Imperial troop transporter (story booklet)|Imperial troop transporter story booklet]] **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] *[[Dark Empire]] }} {{Quote|Artoo's sensors have picked up a ''vehicle'' in the area, sir. ''Larger'' than a land speeder... ''smaller'' than a Jawa sandcrawler. That sounds distressingly like an Imperial ''troop carrier'' to me, sir!|C-3PO|[[Star Wars (1977) 31|''Star Wars'' (1977) 31]]}} The '''Reconnaissance Troop Transporter''' ('''RTT'''), or '''Imperial Troop Transporter''' and '''Imperial Troop Transport''', was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] troop [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]]. ==Characteristics== [[File:RTT_variants.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A transporter variant with its internal troop complement]] The RTT was an armored [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicle built with three open-air [[Traveling Rack|traveling racks]] on each side and usually mounted a twin light fire-linked [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]] on top. The traveling racks could spring open for rapid deployment of the troops carried, but also left the troopers exposed while traveling. In addition to the six troopers carried on the outside, the Troop Transporter had room for several individuals on the inside. At the back of the Transporter was a [[Droid Prisoner Compartment]] used to detain and tame renegade droids. The vehicles were also equipped with [[Prisoner Immobilization Unit]]s to subdue prisoners. ==History== In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Empire used the Troop Transporter under the command of [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] agent [[Mar Barezz]] to attempt to capture [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] recruits, although they escaped capture.''[[Rookies: Rendezvous]]''
[[File:9trooptransporter.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge's]] transporter on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]]]
In addition to patrol work, the RTT also saw action with elite units participating in search-and-destroy missions. Such was the case during the search for the stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]], where search parties on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] used at least one such vehicle to travel across the desert. These troops detained and questioned a band of Jawas suspected of having captured the droids carrying the plans, before killing them and destroying their [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]] with the Transporter's cannon. Several Transporters were later used by [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]] on the same planet, during a test of his [[Silas Tagge/Legends|brother's]] latest invention, the [[Omega Frost/Legends|Omega Frost]]. Sienar's Transporters also participated in the [[Imperial Civil War]] around [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], when they shuttled troops around the battlefields on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']] ==Behind the scenes== The Reconnaissance Troop Transporter originated as a [[1979]] [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] toy called the [[W:c:swmerchandise:Imperial Troop Transporter (39290)|Imperial Troop Transporter]], the first ''Star Wars'' toy to not actually appear in the film. It also came with a [[Imperial troop transporter (story booklet)|story booklet]] that explained that it was present off-screen in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] (then known as simply ''Star Wars''), listing it as being responsible for the destruction of the sandcrawler that had carried [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. It was quickly adapted into fiction that same year in [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Star Wars (1977) 31|''Star Wars'' 31]]. [[File:Troop_Transporter_SWGalaxy4-2009-Card_41.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A Troop Transporter crew captures Princess Leia in trading card art]] The original release of the Imperial Troop Transporter included six sound clips taken from ''A New Hope'', four sound effects and two voice recordings: "R2-D2, where are you?" and "There's one! Set for stun!" The toy was later re-released heavily modified as the [[W:c:swmerchandise:Imperial Cruiser (93351)|Imperial Cruiser]], where most of its electronics were removed and some of its decals were altered. The space that had held the electronics was turned into a cargo hold. A [[2009 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 4]] trading card featured art by [[Matt Busch]] portraying an "imaginary scenario" of stormtroopers traveling in a Troop Transporter having captured [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Princess Leia]]. The princess is shown wearing a Prisoner Immobilization Unit over her head. The design of the vehicle matches the toy design exactly.{{Topps|set=2009 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 4|cardname=Abducting a Princess|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/75146/cid/6271156/2009-Topps-Star-Wars-Galaxy-4-41-Abducting-a-Princess}} ==Appearances== *[[Dark Times—A Spark Remains 3|''Dark Times—A Spark
